stable.notes:
### Added
- **Stop button in the GUI.** A "Stop HubScan server" control on every GUI page
  (`POST /quit`) flags a graceful uvicorn shutdown so a consultant can quit from
  the browser instead of finding the terminal for `Ctrl+C`. `hubscan gui` now
  owns the `uvicorn.Server` and passes an `on_quit` callback into `create_app`
  (optional, so tests/selftest run without a real server). New template:
  `gui_stopped.html.j2`; shared `_stop.html.j2` partial.
- **Guided first-run setup in the GUI.** `hubscan gui` now onboards a new
  consultant without hand-editing files: when no license is found it shows a
  paste-your-key step (saved via new `licensing.save_license`, signature-verified
  before writing); once licensed but with no portals it shows an add-portal step
  with a collapsible HubSpot Private App token guide (the read-only scope list)
  and a form that writes the portal to `portals.toml` via new
  `config.upsert_portal`. The writer uses `tomlkit` so existing comments, key
  order, and advanced tables (`prices`, `thresholds`, `user_overrides`,
  `new_model`) are preserved, and writes atomically. A returning, configured
  laptop skips the wizard and lands on the normal scan UI, which gains an
  "+ Add another portal" link (`GET /portals/new`). New routes: `POST
  /setup/license`, `GET /portals/new`, `POST /portals`. New templates:
  `gui_setup_license.html.j2`, `gui_setup_portal.html.j2`. New dependency:
  `tomlkit`.
- **Update notice in the GUI.** The GUI now surfaces the notify-don't-force
  release check (`update.check`) as a banner on every page (scan UI + the
  onboarding steps): "HubScan X is available", a download/upgrade link, and the
  release notes. Until now only the CLI `scan`/`probe` printed it to stderr, and
  the GUI even suppressed it in the scan subprocess -- so GUI-only consultants
  never saw new releases. Fully fail-soft and cached (no network on most page
  loads). Shared `_update_banner.html.j2` partial; the GUI Jinja loader now
  supports `{% include %}` (still bundles in the frozen binary).
- **Cross-pillar health score + prioritised findings.** New `healthscore.py`
  rolls the hygiene pillars into a single 0-100 number (band-points average:
  green 100 / amber 70 / red 30, meaned per pillar then across pillars) plus a
  severity-ranked top-findings list. Data Quality drives it today; Automation +
  Access join as built; Licensing stays a separate EUR-savings headline (cost,
  not hygiene). Surfaced as a Health-score section in the markdown + HTML reports
  and a `health score: N/100` CLI line. Pure logic over already-measured pillar
  results (re-scores without re-scanning). The per-signal row builder moved to
  `scanners/dataquality.py:signal_rows` (shared by the report + scorer).
- **Data Quality pillar (second audit pillar).** `scanners/dataquality.py` adds a
  read-only, count-only hygiene scan across contacts, companies, deals, tickets,
  **and custom objects**: records with no owner, **records owned by deactivated
  users** (stranded records), stale records (no modification in N days, via
  `hs_lastmodifieddate`), missing critical fields
  (per-object sets), and custom-property sprawl. Each signal degrades on a
  missing scope; 0-record objects are marked "not used". A new **Data Quality**
  section in the markdown + HTML reports shows each signal's count, %, and a
  RED/AMBER/GREEN band (cut-offs tunable via `[<portal>.dq_thresholds]`). The
  numeric pillar score is deferred to the future cross-pillar health score;
  duplicate detection is left to HubSpot's native tools (Breeze). Adds the
  `crm.objects.companies.read` + `tickets` scopes (tickets read is the bare
  `tickets` scope — there is no `crm.objects.tickets.read`). See
  `internal-docs/ROADMAP_data_quality.md`.
- **Licence-issuance playbook + issuer `list` command.** New
  `internal-docs/LICENSE_ISSUANCE_PLAYBOOK.md` (issue → deliver → track → renew,
  with an email template) for onboarding partners repeatably. `tools/
  issue_license.py` gains `list` — a ledger summary showing the current licence
  per partner with expiry, days left, and status (valid / expiring ≤30d /
  EXPIRED), flagging any that need attention. Both vendor-internal.

### Changed
- **Support contact is now `info@hubscan.nl`** (was `martin@emlx.nl`). Updated
  across the EULA, QUICKSTART, README, and the `SUPPORT_CONTACT` shown in CLI
  license-error text. The v0.3.0 release assets were re-uploaded with the new
  address.
- **Corrected the credential terminology: Service Keys, not Private Apps.** A
  Service Key (**Settings → Integrations → Service Keys**, Beta) is a *distinct*
  credential from a Private App access token — earlier docs wrongly called them
  interchangeable. Fixed the QUICKSTART setup step, the `--token` CLI help, the
  README, and the PROBE_FINDINGS reference. Verified live that a Service Key
  grants every required scope (Pietboon passes all 15 probes).

### Fixed
- **`content` scope was missing from the onboarding/scope list.** The marketing-
  email send-volume signal (`/marketing/v3/emails/statistics/list`, new-pricing
  credit driver) needs the `content` scope; it was documented in the
  endpoint-detail section of `PROBE_FINDINGS.md` but absent from the consolidated
  required-scope list. Added there and to the GUI token guide.
- **QUICKSTART macOS bypass steps corrected for recent macOS (Sequoia).** The
  Gatekeeper block now offers only "Move to Trash"/"Done" (no "Open"), so the old
  right-click→Open advice was wrong. Doc now leads with the `xattr -d
  com.apple.quarantine` Terminal steps and the System Settings → Privacy &
  Security → "Open Anyway" path, and uses the real downloaded filenames.
